info.title: Organization API
info.description:
The Organization API provides endpoints for managing epilot tenant organizations.

## Overview

This API allows you to:
- **Retrieve organization details** - Get information about the current organization or a specific organization by ID
- **Update organization profiles** - Modify organization name, contact information, address, and branding assets
- **Manage organization settings** - Configure organization-wide settings such as feature flags and preferences
- **Branding configuration** - Set organization logos, email signatures, and style settings

## Organization Types

Organizations can be configured as:
- **Production** - Live production environments for real business operations
- **Sandbox** - Test environments linked to a parent production organization

tags.name: Organization
tags.description:
Endpoints for managing organization profiles and information.

Organizations represent tenant accounts in the epilot platform. Each organization has:
- Basic profile information (name, contact details, address)
- Branding assets (logo, email signature)
- Configuration settings
- Usage type (Production or Sandbox)

tags.name: Organization Settings
tags.description:
Endpoints for managing organization-wide configuration settings.

Settings are stored as key-value pairs and can include:
- Feature flags (e.g., double_opt_in)
- Integration configurations
- Platform preferences

Settings values can be strings, numbers, booleans, arrays, or objects.

tags.name: Cleanup
tags.description:
Internal endpoints for organization cleanup operations.

These endpoints are used by internal services to manage the deletion and cleanup
of organization data across the platform. They track cleanup status per service
to ensure complete data removal.

tags.name: Internal
tags.description:
Internal endpoints for platform administration.

These endpoints are not part of the public API and are used by internal epilot
services for administrative operations such as organization provisioning,
data aggregation, and cross-organization queries.

tags.name: HubSpot
tags.description:
Internal endpoints for HubSpot CRM integration.

These endpoints provide access to HubSpot company data synchronized with the
epilot data warehouse for organization mapping and CRM integration purposes.

tags.name: Feature Settings
tags.description:
Feature flag metadata for the organization settings page.

This metadata is used by frontend applications to render feature toggles
without hardcoding titles, descriptions, badges, and visibility rules in the UI.

get.description:
Retrieves the organization associated with the authenticated user's current session.

This endpoint is useful for fetching organization details without knowing the organization ID,
as it automatically resolves the organization from the authentication token.

200.description: The organization associated with the current authenticated user

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing authentication token

403.description: Forbidden - User does not have permission to access organization data

get.description:
Retrieves detailed information about a specific organization by its unique identifier.

Returns the organization's profile information including name, contact details,
address, branding assets, and configuration.

parameters.name: org_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the organization to retrieve

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing authentication token

403.description: Forbidden - User does not have permission to access this organization

404.description: Not Found - Organization with the specified ID does not exist

patch.description:
Updates an organization's profile information.

This endpoint supports partial updates - only the fields provided in the request body
will be modified. Fields not included in the request will retain their current values.

Common use cases:
- Updating contact information (email, phone, website)
- Changing the organization name
- Updating the postal address
- Setting or updating branding assets (logo_url, logo_thumbnail_url)
- Configuring the default email signature

parameters.name: org_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the organization to update

requestBody.description: Organization fields to update. Only provided fields will be modified.

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid request body or field values

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing authentication token

403.description: Forbidden - User does not have permission to update this organization

404.description: Not Found - Organization with the specified ID does not exist

get.description:
Retrieves all configuration settings for an organization.

Returns a key-value map of all settings configured for the organization.
Settings control various platform features and behaviors including:
- Feature flags (e.g., double_opt_in, email_tracking)
- Integration configurations
- UI preferences
- Workflow settings

parameters.name: org_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the organization

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing authentication token

403.description: Forbidden - User does not have permission to access organization settings

404.description: Not Found - Organization with the specified ID does not exist

put.description:
Creates or updates a specific organization setting identified by its key.

The setting value can be any valid JSON type:
- String (e.g., "en", "production")
- Number (e.g., 100, 3.14)
- Boolean (e.g., true, false)
- Object (e.g., {"enabled": true, "threshold": 50})
- Array (e.g., ["email", "sms", "push"])

If the setting key does not exist, it will be created. If it exists, its value
will be replaced with the new value.

parameters.name: org_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the organization

parameters.name: key
parameters.description:
The setting key to create or update.
Common keys include: double_opt_in, email_tracking, default_language, workflow_notifications

requestBody.description: The value to set for the specified setting key

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing authentication token

403.description: Forbidden - User does not have permission to modify organization settings

404.description: Not Found - Organization with the specified ID does not exist

delete.description:
Removes a specific organization setting identified by its key.

After deletion, the setting will no longer appear in the organization's settings
and any features relying on this setting will use their default behavior.

parameters.name: org_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the organization

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing authentication token

403.description: Forbidden - User does not have permission to delete organization settings

404.description: Not Found - Organization or setting key does not exist

get.description:
Returns platform-level configuration metadata including feature flag definitions,
resource type mappings, and error code severities.

This data is static (identical for all organizations) and changes infrequently.
Clients should cache responses using the Cache-Control header.

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing authentication token

EpilotAuth.description: Authorization header with epilot OAuth2 bearer token

OrganizationId.description: Unique identifier for an organization (tenant) in the epilot platform

Organization.description:
Represents an epilot organization (tenant).

An organization contains all the configuration, branding, and contact information
for a tenant account on the epilot platform.

organization_use.description:
Indicates whether this is a production or sandbox organization.
Sandbox organizations are linked to a parent production organization for testing purposes.

parent_production_org_id.description:
The ID of the parent production organization.
Only set for sandbox organizations to link them to their production counterpart.

pricing_tier.description: Deprecated - Please use pricing tier api

tags.description:
Labels/tags associated with the organization for categorization and filtering.
Used internally to classify organizations (e.g., "enterprise", "beta-tester", "test org").

InternalOrganization.description:
Extended organization object with internal-only fields.

Includes all fields from the base Organization schema plus additional
internal metadata used for platform administration and support.

SettingKey.description:
A unique key identifying an organization setting.
Common setting keys include: double_opt_in, email_tracking, default_language, workflow_notifications

Settings.description:
A key-value map of all organization settings.
Keys are setting identifiers and values can be any valid JSON type.

SettingsValue.description:
The value of an organization setting.
Can be any valid JSON type: string, number, boolean, array, or object.

CreateOrganizationRequest.description: Request payload for creating a new organization

organization_detail.description: Core organization information for the new tenant

email_address.description: Primary contact email for the organization

type.description:
Organization type classification.
- Vendor: Primary organization type for epilot customers
- Partner: Partner organization type

organization_use.description:
Whether this is a production or sandbox environment.
Sandbox organizations are for testing and linked to a production org.

pricing_tier_id.description:
The ULID of the pricing tier to assign to this organization.
Determines feature access and billing.

owner_user.description:
Initial owner user details.
This user will receive an invitation email to complete registration
and become the organization administrator.

full_name.description: Full name of the owner user

email_address.description: Email address for the invitation (must be unique in the platform)

DataPointsResponse.description: List of data point metrics for all organizations

DataPoint.description: Data point metrics for a single organization, used for usage tracking and billing

max_customer.description:
Maximum number of customer entities recorded in the previous month.
Used as a high-water mark for billing calculations.

actual_customer.description:
Current count of customer entities in the organization.
Represents the actual usage at the time of the query.

OrganizationToCleanup.description:
Represents an organization that has been marked for deletion and requires cleanup.
Contains metadata about the deletion request.

deleted_at.description: Timestamp when the organization was marked for deletion

deleted_by.description: User ID of the person who initiated the deletion

OrganizationCleanupStatus.description:
Records the cleanup status reported by a specific service for an organization.
Each service that stores organization data reports its cleanup operations here.

service_name.description: Name of the service reporting its cleanup status

operations.description:
List of cleanup operations performed by the service.
Each operation describes what action was taken on which resource.

action.description:
The type of cleanup action performed.
Common values: Delete, Archive, Purge

resource.description:
The resource that was cleaned up.
Can be a table name, S3 bucket, or other resource identifier.

extra_info.description: Additional context about the cleanup operation

HubspotCompany.description:
Represents a company record from HubSpot CRM synchronized to the epilot data warehouse.
Used for CRM integration and organization mapping.

company_name.description: The display name of the company in HubSpot

domain.description: The primary web domain associated with the company

HubspotCompaniesResponse.description: Response containing a list of HubSpot companies matching the search criteria

results.description: List of HubSpot companies matching the query

total.description: Total number of companies in the response

HubspotOrganizationData.description:
HubSpot company data associated with an epilot organization.
Contains business metrics and CRM properties synced from HubSpot.

company_name.description: The display name of the company in HubSpot

domain.description: The primary web domain associated with the company

properties.description: Full HubSpot properties object with all available fields

FeatureSettings.description: Feature settings metadata served to frontend applications.

feature_flags.description: Feature flag metadata for the settings page

FeatureFlagMetadata.description: Metadata for a single feature flag toggle

feature_name.description: Unique identifier matching the organization setting key

badge.description: Badge label shown next to the feature toggle

confetti.description: Whether to show confetti animation when enabled

one_way.description: Whether the toggle can only be turned on (not off)

reverse_checked.description: Whether the toggle value is inverted (checked = disabled)

is_visible_for_partner.description: Whether this feature is visible for partner organizations

visibility_rules.description: Rules that must ALL pass for this feature to be visible (implicit AND)

I18nString.description: Internationalized string with a translation key and optional default value

default_value.description: Fallback value when translation is not available

VisibilityRule.description:
A rule that determines feature visibility. Rules are combined with AND logic
at the top level. Supports boolean combinators (and, or, not) for complex logic.

setting_key.description: Setting key for pricing_tier and setting_enabled rules

flag_name.description: Flag name for feature_flag and flag_enabled rules
